howToUse.macros: Calorie, protein, carb, fat, fiber, sugar, saturated fat, and sodium are estimates calculated from standard ingredient portions unless verifiedMacros is true. Treat them as close working figures.
howToUse.micros: Exact vitamin and mineral values are not published for these meals. For real micronutrient tracking, log each meal using the ingredients array below. Your app already has verified USDA data for those whole foods and will compute the micros for you. The microHighlights field names the standouts each meal genuinely delivers based on its ingredients.
howToUse.sodiumNote: Sodium is the micronutrient that matters most for restaurant food and is the hardest to control. Estimates are included and high-sodium items are flagged.

needFromYou: Name of the taco shop on Yorba Linda Blvd that starts with B, so I can add it.
Which Higo in La Habra: Higo Chicken (Peruvian rotisserie) or Higo Sushi Peruvian Fusion. Catalog currently uses the Peruvian rotisserie picks.

foods.name: Charbroiled Chicken Shish Kabob
foods.serving: 1 kabob plate, rice swapped for extra salad
microHighlights: Niacin, B6, and selenium from chicken
Vitamin K, A, and folate from the greens
Potassium from vegetables
foods.orderTip: Swap the basmati for a second salad to cut the carbs. Best protein-to-calorie order on your whole list.

foods.name: Charbroiled Salmon Salad
microHighlights: Vitamin D, B12, and omega-3s from salmon
Potassium, vitamin E, and folate from avocado
Vitamin K and A from romaine
Calcium from tzatziki
foods.orderTip: Easy on the avocado if trimming fat. Tzatziki on the side.

foods.name: Chicken Salad with Spicy Ranch
microHighlights: B vitamins and selenium from chicken
Calcium from cheddar
Choline and selenium from egg
Vitamin A and K from lettuce, vitamin C from tomato
foods.orderTip: Lean build: skip bacon, halve cheese, 1 tbsp ranch lands ~520 cal, 63g protein. Watch the sodium.

foods.name: Grilled Chicken Salad
foods.serving: 1 salad, 6 oz charbroiled chicken, light dressing

ingredients.item: romaine, cucumber, radish, red onion, tomato

microHighlights: B vitamins and selenium from chicken
Potassium and vitamin E from avocado
Calcium from jack cheese
Vitamin C from tomato, fiber and magnesium from corn
foods.orderTip: Charbroiled, dressing on the side, easy on the chips.

foods.name: Charbroiled Fish Tacos
microHighlights: Selenium, B12, and iodine from fish
Fiber and magnesium from corn tortillas
Vitamin C and K from cabbage
foods.orderTip: Always charbroiled, never the beer-battered version.

foods.name: Grilled Chicken Tacos
foods.serving: 3 tacos, a la carte on handmade corn tortillas
microHighlights: B vitamins and selenium from chicken
Fiber and magnesium from corn masa
Vitamin C and lycopene from salsa
foods.orderTip: Grilled proteins over fried, salsas instead of crema. Carne asada is a fattier swap.

foods.name: Salmon Kale Salad
microHighlights: Vitamin D, B12, and omega-3s from salmon
Vitamin K, A, C, and calcium from kale
foods.orderTip: Dressing on the side.

foods.name: Ceviche
microHighlights: Selenium and B12 from fish
Vitamin C from lime and tomato
foods.orderTip: Leanest high-protein option here. Skip the fried plantain or chips it may come with.

foods.name: Organic Green Salad with Grilled Salmon
microHighlights: Vitamin D, B12, omega-3s, and selenium from salmon
Magnesium, zinc, and iron from pepitas
Vitamin K, A, and folate from greens
foods.orderTip: Swap chicken or shrimp for salmon if you prefer. Dressing on the side, chips off the table.

foods.name: Chicken Fajitas
foods.serving: 1 order, protein and veg focus, light on starch
microHighlights: B vitamins from chicken
Heavy vitamin C from bell peppers
Folate, fiber, and iron from black beans
foods.orderTip: Eat the protein and the peppers and onions. Keep the rice and tortillas light. Sodium runs high.

foods.name: Shrimp Ceviche or Cocktail
microHighlights: Selenium, B12, and iodine from shrimp
Vitamin C and lycopene from tomato
Potassium from avocado
foods.orderTip: Very lean, high protein. Skip the chips served alongside.

foods.name: Double-Double Protein Style
microHighlights: B12, zinc, and iron from beef
Calcium from cheese
foods.orderTip: Protein Style swaps the bun for lettuce and cuts almost all the carbs. Spread is the fat lever, so go light or sub mustard and ketchup. High saturated fat and sodium, so this is a treat-day order. Skip the fries.

foods.name: 4x4 Protein Style
microHighlights: B12, zinc, and iron from beef
Calcium from cheese
foods.orderTip: Big protein hit but very high saturated fat and sodium. Order only on a low-fat day elsewhere.

foods.name: Modern Caesar with Chicken
foods.serving: 1 salad, dressing on side, add chicken
microHighlights: Vitamin K and A from kale and romaine
Calcium from parmesan
Potassium and vitamin E from avocado
B vitamins from chicken
foods.orderTip: Dressing on the side, skip the krispies. Avoid the Summer GOAT salad except as a treat (750 cal, only ~34g protein, see food-database entry).

foods.name: Grill Bowl with Chicken (Chimichurri or Teriyaki)
foods.serving: 1 bowl, half rice or greens base
microHighlights: B vitamins and selenium from chicken
Vitamin C, A, and K from vegetables
foods.orderTip: Chimichurri is lower sugar than teriyaki. Sub greens for half the rice to drop carbs.

foods.name: Baja Cali Tacos with Salmon
microHighlights: Vitamin D, B12, and omega-3s from salmon
Vitamin C and K from cabbage
Fiber from corn tortillas
foods.orderTip: Grilled salmon, sauce light.

foods.name: Peruvian Rotisserie Chicken (Pollo a la Brasa)
foods.serving: 1/4 chicken, salad instead of fries, aji on side
microHighlights: Niacin, B6, selenium, and phosphorus from chicken
Vitamin A and K from the salad
foods.orderTip: Salad or extra veg instead of fries or rice. Remove the skin to cut fat further. Aji sauce on the side.

foods.name: Peruvian Ceviche

ingredients.item: halibut or mixed seafood cured in lime

microHighlights: Selenium, B12, and iodine from fish
Vitamin C from lime and chiles
foods.orderTip: Leanest high-protein pick. Limit the choclo (corn) and sweet potato sides if cutting.

foods.name: Lomo Saltado
microHighlights: Iron, B12, and zinc from beef
Vitamin C and potassium from tomato and onion
foods.orderTip: Comes loaded with fries and rice. Eat the beef and vegetables first and treat the starch as a side. Higher carb and sodium.

foods.name: PLACEHOLDER, name needed
foods.source: Taco shop on Yorba Linda Blvd starting with B
foods.orderTip: Tell me the name and your usual order and I will fill this in. As a default at any taco shop, grilled chicken or carne asada tacos on corn, salsa over crema, skip the chips.
